features.name: Unified Dashboarding
features.description: Consolidates logs, CPU/memory performance metrics, and application traces in real-time dashboards.

features.name: Dynamic Alerting Rules
features.description: Triggers email, Slack, or PagerDuty alerts automatically when metric thresholds are breached.

features.name: Distributed Trace Analysis
features.description: Tracks request execution paths across multiple microservices to pinpoint latency bottlenecks.

iamRoles.description: Full access to configure monitoring dashboards, alerting policies, and uptime checks.
Permissions to configure log sinks, buckets, retention policies, and view all logs.

cliExamples.description: Create a log routing sink to export logs to a Cloud Storage bucket
cliExamples.command:
gcloud logging sinks create error-reporting-sink \
  storage.googleapis.com/my-log-bucket \
  --log-filter="severity>=ERROR"

cliExamples.description: List all active alerting policies in the project

design: Google Cloud Observability (formerly Stackdriver) provides logging, monitoring, tracing, and profiling.
Cloud Monitoring collects performance metrics from GCP services, VMs, and custom applications.
Cloud Logging ingests, filters, and routes log lines from applications and audit trails.
Log Sinks route filtered logs to Cloud Storage (archival), BigQuery (analytics), or Pub/Sub (real-time stream).
Cloud Trace analyses request latency across microservices, identifying performance bottlenecks.
Cloud Profiler continuously analyzes CPU and memory allocation patterns inside application code.
Uptime checks monitor external endpoints, alerting operators on availability failures.
Use MQL (Monitoring Query Language) to write advanced metric aggregation and filtering queries.
security: Administrative changes and data access logs are recorded in immutable Admin Activity and Data Access Audit Logs.
Exclude sensitive PII or credentials from logs using Logging exclusions or Sensitive Data Protection.
Manage log access using IAM logging roles (`roles/logging.privateViewer` is required to view audit logs).
Encrypt log buckets at rest using Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) via Cloud KMS.
Access to Cloud Monitoring dashboards can be restricted to security and operations teams.
Log sinks can export logs to a secure, isolated audit project bucket for compliance.
VPC Service Controls perimeters protect metrics and trace data from external exfiltration.
Set strict log bucket retention periods to comply with data privacy regulations (e.g. GDPR).
reliability: Cloud Logging is serverless, scaling instantly to absorb massive spikes in system logs.
Log routing sinks support automated retries to ensure log delivery to destinations (Storage, BigQuery).
Configure alert notifications to multiple channels (PagerDuty, SMS, Slack, Webhooks) to ensure response.
Uptime checks run from multiple geographical locations worldwide to prevent false positives.
Trace and Profiler agents run in the background with low overhead, ensuring application stability.
Replicate log buckets across regions to secure logs from regional disasters.
Store historical logs in Cloud Storage buckets with Object Lifecycle Lock for WORM compliance.
Create custom metric alerts to detect anomalies in log ingestion rates.
cost: Cloud Logging charges per GB of logs ingested and for logs retained beyond the default 30 days.
Cloud Monitoring charges based on custom metrics ingested and API read/write operations.
Reduce logging costs by setting up Log Exclusions to drop high-volume, low-value debug logs.
Configure log sinks to only route warnings and errors to BigQuery, keeping debug logs in standard storage.
Shorten log bucket retention periods to the minimum required to avoid monthly log storage fees.
Monitor custom metric ingestion rates; avoid exporting high-cardinality metrics (like user IDs) to Monitoring.
Standard GCP service metrics are ingested at no cost; only custom or Prometheus metrics incur fees.
Use Cloud Storage Archive class for long-term log storage to minimize GB/month fees.
